outcomeMetrics.name: Days exceeding Air Quality Index standards

topRecommendations: Military: US spends $2052/cap (rank 23/23). Ireland spends $226/cap with Life Expectancy 82. Overspend: 7.4x. Potential savings: $601B/yr
Medicaid: US spends $10333/cap (rank 23/23). Czech Republic spends $2898/cap with Infant Survival (inverted mortality) 97.6. Overspend: 3.5x. Potential savings: $2.5T/yr
Science / NASA: US spends $1991/cap (rank 20/23). Ireland spends $859/cap with GDP per Capita 75433.33. Overspend: 2.4x. Potential savings: $389B/yr
Social Security: US spends $12848/cap (rank 22/23). South Korea spends $6037/cap with Life Expectancy 83.57. Overspend: 2x. Potential savings: $2.2T/yr
Education: US spends $2898/cap (rank 8/23). Near floor ($2572/cap). GDP per Capita: 57590

inflationAdjustment.note: Matches OECD cross-country PPP convention for comparable analysis

methodology.oslMethod: Diminishing returns curve fitting (log-linear or saturation model)
methodology.oslThreshold: OSL where marginal return drops to 50% of cross-country average
methodology.dataClamping: OSL clamped to [50% min, 150% max] of observed cross-country spending
methodology.lowFitGuard: Models with R² < 0.3 constrained to [0.5×, 2×] current spending
methodology.nonDiscretionary: Social Security, Medicare, Interest on Debt, Other Mandatory excluded from optimization

note: Budget analysis uses real OECD cross-country data (23 countries × 23 years) for OSL estimation where available. Categories without OECD mappings use outcome-trend heuristics.
